codeReview.summary: The implementation adds comprehensive structured logging to all auth handlers with consistent `[auth] operation=X key=value` format. The `hashEmail()` helper properly protects PII by using 8-char SHA-256 prefix hashes. All 5 auth handler operations (register begin/finish, login begin/finish, logout, session refresh) now have detailed logging with appropriate context for debugging.

issues.description: Sign count update failure logs at 'warning' level but this is still an error condition. Consider making this more prominent or alerting-worthy since it could indicate a replay attack.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed the structured logging pattern established in AGENTS.md: `log.Printf("[auth] operation: %s, user_id: %s", op, userID)` format. The implementation extends this with additional context fields.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handlers.go lines 398-960 - all log lines use consistent `[auth] operation=X key=value` format as specified in AGENTS.md Coding Conventions section

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed go-worker skill procedure precisely. Wrote tests before implementation (TDD pattern visible in transcript), ran validators, and committed with descriptive message.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: test file creation (logging_test.go) with 26 comprehensive tests, then implementation in handlers.go, then validator runs (go test, go vet, gofmt). Handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services needed - logging uses standard Go log package which writes to stderr, captured by systemd/journalctl on Node B.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No changes to .factory/services.yaml required. Logs are visible via `journalctl -u go-choir-auth -f` as documented in AGENTS.md.

summary: This is a high-quality implementation of structured logging for auth handlers. The code is well-structured, thoroughly tested (26 new tests), and follows all established conventions. The email hashing approach (8-char SHA-256 prefix) provides good privacy protection while still allowing correlation across log lines. All expected behaviors from the feature definition are met: registration/login attempts logged, failures logged with detail, credential operations logged, sign counter updates logged, and challenge state operations logged. No sensitive data (raw emails, credentials, keys) appears in logs. Ready for user testing.
